title: House Tresario

{{Quote|The Guild consists of 10 major houses: the pre-eminent House Salaktori, the females-only House Renliss, House Benelex, newcomers House Neuvalis, House Paramexor, House Tresario, the Mantis Syndicate, the Ragnar Syndicate, Skine Bounty Hunter College and the Slaver Syndicate.|Commander Scay Danson, ISB|<ref name="INS" />}}
'''House Tresario''' was an organization of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] founded in [[8 BBY/Legends|8 BBY]] by former [[Officer/Legends|officers]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] to track down [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. When they discovered that it was a profitable venture, they officially created a chapter of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]], specializing in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] bounties on raiders and in [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] exceeding 14,999 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], House Tresario was a new bounty hunter chapter, not as influential as others, but still profitable. It retained links with active Imperial officers, and was opposed by [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]] affiliated to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Funding of House Tresario came from [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Clezo]] of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]]. House Tresario had a rivalry with [[House Benelex]], another active bounty hunter chapter.

==Organization==
House Tresario, sometimes known as House Tresario Bounty Hunters Guild, was a corporation of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. Tresario employed a number of professional, licensed hunters, accepted [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] postings, and then paired one of its hunter, or several hunters, with each bounty. It was one of the chapters of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="G101" /><ref name="INS" /><ref name="TFUCG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

House Tresario was specifically founded to track [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]<ref name="G101" /><ref name="TFUCG" /><ref name="PP">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref> that harassed the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]].<ref name="G101" /><ref name="PP" /> In time, they modified their activities to accept simply any bounty greater than 15,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], no matter the charges against the fugitive.<ref name="G101" /> Even then, House Tresario still specialized in Imperial contracts on raiders.<ref name="PP" /> Tresario's hunters operated through all the space controlled by the Galactic Empire, but a 22% of their activities took place either in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]'' or in surrounding areas. The main headquarters of the House were on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Baradas II]],<ref name="G101" /> in [[Colonies/Legends|the Colonies]]<ref name="atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

A prospective member wanting to join House Tresario had to be sponsored by two or more bounty hunters already belonging to House Tresario. A member of the House had to pay 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to the House as an annual fee, plus an 8% of the net value of any obtained bounty. In exchange, House Tresario would provide the members with employment and with training in their area of specialization. The so-called Tresario hunter-improvement programs were mocked by [[House Benelex]], a rival bounty hunter chapter, as this training was perceived as useless.<ref name="G101" />

==History==
[[Rovan Tresario]], a charismatic [[Noble/Legends|aristocrat]] and [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], was angered at the problem posed by pirates. He retired from the Navy, joined other former Imperial officers who shared his dislike, and together, hired a number of bounty hunters to track a number of pirates whom they personally hated. The pirates were captured, and Tresario and his partners even obtained a profit. They decided to continue with this activity and go after bigger bounties every year. In [[8 BBY/Legends|8 BBY]], House Tresario was officially founded by Rovan Tresario and the other ex-Imperial officers.<ref name="G101" /><ref name="SOTE">''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref>

[[1 ABY/Legends|Seven years later]], House Tresario was considered a new, expanding House with moderate influence through the Empire. Rovan Tresario was still the CEO of the House. At that time, they used the services of 9,659 non-hunting members for management tasks, plus 1,767 bounty hunters, including former warden [[Garim Ayrvn]]. Ayrvn had made a specific deal with House Tresario to obtain help and equipment in exchange for an important share of any bounty he could obtain, instead of paying the usual annual fee and gap.<ref name="G101" /> Around that time, House Tresario was mentioned in a report by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Scay Danson]] of [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] about the Bounty Hunter's Guild and other fringe activities.<ref name="INS" />

During these years, House Tresario found a rival in another bounty hunter chapter, House Benelex. [[Corvastan Benelex]] publicly ridiculed Tresario's training activities and called it a social club. House Tresario in turn disapproved the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]] and considered that Benelex should not profit from tracking targets of [[Legal Authorization for Advanced Confinement Document]]s, an ''ad hoc'' legal instrument to convict the enemies of the Inquisition even without charges.<ref name="G101" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], House Tresario was at least partially funded by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Clezo]], one of the [[Vigo/Legends|Vigos]] in the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] criminal organization. Clezo was known to fund other organizations of bounty hunters at that point, as well as being involved in many criminal activities<ref name="SOTE" />

Having been founded by Imperial officers, House Tresario retained important links to the Imperial Navy,<ref name="SOTE" /> and hunted pirates for the benefit of the Empire. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the anti-Imperial [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] used the services of pirates as [[Privateer/Legends|privateers]], and these buccaneers became natural enemies of the Imperial bounty hunters. Considering that House Tresario was specialized in working for the Empire, not infrequently against Alliance members, Tresario was added to the list of legitimate target for Alliance privateers, the [[Alliance Target List]]. However, only the best among the Alliance privateers were courageous enough to attack [[Starship/Legends|starships]] and assets of a bounty hunter house.<ref name="PP" />

==Behind the scenes==
House Tresario was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' ([[1994]]), a [[Roleplaying game|role-playing]] guide written by [[Rick D. Stuart]]. Information on it was expanded in further role-playing guides, including ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' ([[1996]]) and ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'' ([[1997]]). It was mentioned in passing in the article ''[[Underworld: A Galaxy of Scum and Villainy]]'' ([[2006]]).
